banishing_warden.name: Banishing Warden
description: ## Banishing Warden
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/18›
The banishing warden is an expert at fighting all types of evil outsiders and sending them back to their native planes.
::cskill{gain=kp lose=kno}

::ab[Smite Evil (Su)]{icon=down-boost info="A banishing warden's *smite evil* deals its bonus damage only on the first attack against outsiders (but not dragons or undead). However, it damages any evil outsider, not only those with the evil subtype (including ‹subtype/native› outsiders)." alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Banishing Smite (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic ability="When the banishing warden confirms a critical hit against a target of her *smite evil* effect, she can attempt to forcibly ‹spell/banish<ment› the target to its home plane. The creature struck must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the banishing warden's level + the banishing warden's Charisma modifier) or be sent back to its home plane as per ‹spell/dismissal›." imp10="A creature dismissed in this manner takes an amount of damage equal to 2 points per paladin level at the start of its next turn." useInc=paladin~6~3 useF="3~1~6" special="A banishing warden still receives the benefits of the *mercy* class ability starting at 6th level and onward." replace="3rd-level mercy"}

::ab[Smiting Aura (Su)]{l=11 icon=power ability="When an ally of the banishing warden is affected by her *aura of justice,* that ally can use *banishing smite.* This counts toward the banishing warden's number of uses per day and cannot be activated if the banishing warden has no uses of *banishing smite* remaining." alter="Aura of justice"}

champion_of_the_cascade.name: Champion of the Cascade
description: ## Champion of the Cascade
‹SOURCE Wilderness Origins/30›

Great civilizations may rise and fall, but water will always mark the places where cities grow. Rivers carve trails through the ground, enabling travel and trade and delivering the fresh water necessary to make agrarian society possible. Champions of the cascade are paladins who defend the freedom and purity of waterways, protecting the very existence of the civilizations they belong to.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A champion of the cascade is proficient with light armor, medium armor, and shields (except tower shields)." alter="Armor proficiency"}
::cskill{gain=acro~kg~swim lose=kno}
::ab[Swiftsurge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A champion of the cascade does not take any penalty on Acrobatics or Swim checks due to her armor check penalty." replace="Divine health"}

::ab[Flowing Stride (Su)]{icon=power l3="Whenever a champion of the cascade uses *lay on hands* to restore hit points to one target, she can grant a willing target the ability to walk on water as ‹spell/water walk› for 1 minute." l9="This extends to creatures healed by her *channel positive energy.*" l15="Healed creatures can walk on water for 24 hours." replace=Mercy}

::ab[Rising Tide (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic flavor="A champion of the cascade's connection to her deity allows her to summon rivers and alter her surroundings" useL=paladin useUnit=minute useNC=1 standard="She can change up to two 5-foot squares per paladin level within 100 feet to become freshwater pools with a depth of up to 5 feet per paladin level. The squares must be adjacent to each other. Each creature standing in the area where she first creates the water must attempt a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half the paladin's level + the paladin's Charisma modifier) to jump to the nearest unaffected space and avoid falling into the water." order=usn info="The changed squares revert back to their original form after the duration ends, and any creatures in the water are shunted out to the nearest open space." imp10="As a @HLswift_action, she may spend 1 minute of her *rising tide's* duration to create a geyser in any body of water she has created, lifting an allied creature in that square up to 10 feet per paladin level into the air for 1 round." replace="Divine bond"}

chaos_knight.name: Chaos Knight
description: ## Chaos Knight
‹SOURCE Plane-Hopper's Handbook/18›

Despite the entropic forces inherent in ganzis, some forgo a life of chaos, instead harnessing their heritage to enforce the power of law. The chaos knight is able to tap into the nature of the Maelstrom in order to grant or gain small, unpredictable blessings to aid on her quest for order. The following archetype is available to ganzi characters.
::prereq{r=Ganzi}

::ab[Entropic Touch (Su)]{l=2 icon=protect-def flavor="A chaos knight can employ the forces of entropy with a touch to shatter a mind-affecting spell or effect currently affecting an ally." standard="He can touch a creature to grant that creature a new saving throw against one mind-affecting effect, with a bonus on the save equal to ‹misc/one-third› the chaos knight's level (minimum +0)." special="If the chaos knight targets himself, he can use this ability as a @HLswift_action." usage="A number of times per day equal to half his paladin level + his Charisma modifier" replace="Lay on hands"}

:::ab{l=3 title="Blessings of the Maelstrom (Su)" icon=protect-def-boost flavor="A chaos knight can harness the unpredictable essence of the Maelstrom to bless a creature." action=Ability incrementPlain="He can now maintain the blessing~2~3" incrementDesc=on incrementEnd=" creatures at the same time." replace=Mercy}

When he uses his *entropic touch* ability, the chaos knight can grant the touched creature a blessing from the table below that lasts for a number of rounds equal to 1 + the chaos knight's Charisma modifier (minimum 1 round). The creature rolls 1d4 and compares the result to the table below to determine the blessing. If the chaos knight targets himself with the blessing, he can roll twice and choose his preferred result.
| D4 | Blessing |
| 1 | The creature gains a sacred bonus to AC equal to 1/5 the paladin's level (minimum +1). |
| 2 | The creature gains a sacred bonus on weapon damage rolls equal to the chaos knight's Charisma modifier. |
| 3 | The creature gains ‹umr/fast healing› 2. This fast healing increases by 1 for every 3 levels the chaos knight has. |
| 4 | The creature gains an enhancement bonus to its movement speeds, adding 5 feet of movement for every 2 levels the chaos knight has (to a maximum of +40 feet). |
Only one creature can have this blessing at a time. If another creature gains the blessing, the previous blessing ends.

::ab[Aura of Chaos (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost-lower passive="A chaos knight treats his weapons as chaos-aligned for the purposes of bypassing damage reduction. In addition, the chaos knight treats attacks targeting enemies within 10 feet of him as chaos-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." replace="Aura of faith"}

chosen_one.name: Chosen One
description: ## Chosen One
‹SOURCE Familiar Folio/6›

Most paladins train for years at a temple to attain a holy status, but rarely, an emissary of the divine appears to one of humble origins and calls her directly to the charge. These chosen ones may lack experience, but their teamwork with their emissaries allows them to defeat any evil.
::ab[Bondless]{icon=down info="A chosen one does not gain the *divine bond* class feature."}

::ab[Divine Emissary (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A chosen one gains an ‹arc-familiar/emissary› ‹ability/familiar›, treating her paladin level as her ‹class/wizard› level for the purpose of this ability."}

::ab[Religious Mentor (Ex)]{icon=boost-down-skill flavor="The ‹sidekick/familiar«'s› sworn duty is to help train the chosen one for her future glory." passive="The familiar is treated as having a number of ranks in Knowledge (religion) equal to the chosen one's paladin level." special="The chosen one doesn't gain Knowledge (religion) as a class skill."}

::ab[Delayed Grace (Su)]{flavor="A chosen one begins her adventuring career without fully understanding her true potential." icon=info-down order=pn passive="The chosen one uses the Intuitive column to calculate her typical starting ‹rule/age›." info="She receives the *smite evil* ability at 2nd level and the *divine grace* ability at 4th level. This does not affect the rate at which she gains additional uses per day of *smite evil,* so she still gains her second use at 4th level, her third at 7th level, and so on." alter="Divine grace~smite evil"}

::ab[Lay on Paws (Su)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A chosen one's familiar is able to borrow some of her divine energy to heal itself and others." ability="The familiar can use the chosen one's *lay on hands* ability, including all of her mercies, but each such use consumes two uses of the paladin's *lay on hands* ability." imp4="The familiar can also *channel positive energy,* but each such use consumes four uses of the paladin's *lay on hands* ability." alter="Lay on hands~channel positive energy"}

::ab[True Form (Ex)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A chosen one's familiar reveals its true form, transforming into an outsider ‹ability/improved familiar<s› that matches the chosen one's patron's alignment (typically an ‹monster/arbiter›, a ‹monster/cassisian›, a ‹monster/harbinger›, or a ‹monster/silvanshee›, but potentially any lawful neutral, lawful good, or neutral good outsider familiar depending on the patron). The familiar gains the ‹umr/change shape› universal monster ability if it doesn't already have it, which it can use at will to transform into its original form or back to its true form."}
::ab[Emissary's Smite (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost passive="A chosen one's familiar also benefits from the paladin's *smite evil* ability whenever the chosen one uses *smite evil.*"}

combat_healer_squire.name: Combat Healer Squire
description: ## Combat Healer Squire
‹SOURCE Knights of the Inner Sea/20›
Always ready with bandages and potions, the combat healer squire keeps her knight on his feet against all odds, and always has just the right remedy on hand.

::ab[Careful Healer (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="A combat healer squire provokes no attacks of opportunity when she uses the ‹skill/Heal› skill to stabilize a creature or treat poison. In addition, when she administers long-term care with the Heal skill, she can restore a total number of additional hit points equal to her character level, divided as she wishes among those she is tending." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Dress Wounds (Su)]{l=2 icon=aid ability="A combat healer squire can hastily dress a deadly wound for her knight, temporary alleviating his suffering. This ability acts in all ways like using the Heal skill to treat deadly wounds, except the combat healer squire only needs to take a @HLfull-round_action to use this ability. The hit points healed are temporary, and only last 10 minutes, but they are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›." special="A combat healer squire can only use this ability on her knight, not other creatures. A knight cannot benefit from this ability more than once per day, and cannot be treated for deadly wounds with the Heal skill within 24 hours of being subjected to this ability." replace="Divine grace"}

::ab[Swift Healer (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A combat healer squire becomes even more adept when treating wounds, and can use the Heal skill to perform first aid or treat poison as a @HLswift_action." replace="Divine health"}

divine_defender.name: Divine Defender
description: ## Divine Defender
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/116›

Some paladins see themselves as the last line of defense between the teeming hordes of evil and the innocent folk trying to make a living in a harsh, unforgiving world. These defenders spend their lives protecting others and taking on foes that the common man should not even know exist. To aid them in their holy mission, they have special powers to protect themselves and those around them. The divine defender has the following class features.

::ab[Shared Defense (Su)]{l=3 icon=protect standard="A divine defender can spend one use of her *lay on hands* ability to grant all adjacent allies (including paladins) a bonus. Adjacent allies receive a +1 sacred bonus to their AC and CMD and on their saving throws. These bonuses last for a number of rounds equal to the divine defender's Charisma modifier." increment="The bonus~3~6" imp6="These bonuses are granted to all allies within 10 feet, and allies that are at fewer than 0 hit points within this area are automatically stabilized." imp12="These bonuses are granted to all allies within 15 feet, and allies within this area are immune to ‹umr/bleed› damage." imp18="These bonuses are granted to all allies within 20 feet, and allies within this area gain a 25% chance to negate any sneak attack or critical hit scored against them. This ability does not stack with the chance provided from the light, medium, or heavy fortification armor special abilities." special="These bonuses are cumulative with each other. Allies only benefit from these bonuses while in the listed area." replace=Mercy}

::ab[Divine Bond (Su)]{l=5 icon=def-warning flavor="Instead of forming a *divine bond* with her weapon or a mount, a divine defender can form a bond with her armor." standard="A divine defender can enhance her armor by calling upon the aid of a celestial spirit. This bond lasts for 1 minute per paladin level. When called, the spirit causes the armor to shed light like a ‹eq-misc/torch›. The spirit grants the armor a +1 enhancement bonus." incrementOrd="The armor gains~5~3" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" +1 enhancement bonus." imp8="These bonuses can be added to the armor, stacking with existing armor bonuses to a maximum of +3, or they can be used to add any of the following armor properties: ‹magic-enh/champion›, ‹magic-enh/ghost touch<_a›, ‹magic-enh/heavy fortification›, ‹magic-enh/invulnerability›, ‹magic-enh/light fortification›, ‹magic-enh/moderate fortification›, ‹magic-enh/spell resistance› (13, 15, 17, or 19). Adding these properties consumes an amount of bonus equal to the property's cost. If the armor is not magical, at least a +1 enhancement bonus must be added before any other properties can be added." imp11="In addition, the bonuses can be consumed at the listed amount to add any of the following armor properties: ‹magic-enh/energy resistance› for +3 bonus, ‹magic-enh/improved energy resistance› for +5 bonus, or ‹magic-enh/righteous› for +4 bonus." special="These bonuses are added to any properties the armor already has, but duplicate abilities do not stack. The bonus and properties granted by the spirit are determined when the spirit is called and cannot be changed until the spirit is called again. The celestial spirit imparts no bonuses if the armor is worn by anyone other than the divine defender, but it resumes giving bonuses if the divine defender dons the armor again. If a suit of armor with a celestial spirit is destroyed, the divine defender loses the use of this ability for 30 days, or until she gains a level, whichever comes first. During this 30-day period, the divine defender takes a -1 penalty on attack and weapon damage rolls." useInc=paladin~3~5 useF="5~1~3"}

divine_guardian.name: Divine Guardian
description: ## Divine Guardian
‹SOURCE Cohorts and Companions/23›

The divine guardian devotes herself to the protection and guardianship of one creature at a time, be that her liege lord, an allied cleric, or an innocent facing overwhelming danger at the hands of evil.

::ab[Martial Focus]{icon=down info="The divine guardian devotes herself to the concerns of the material world, eschewing some divine studies. She does not gain access to divine spellcasting and cannot use ‹misc/spell-completion› magic items."}

::ab[Divine Troth (Sp)]{ability="Once per day the divine guardian pledges her protection to a willing creature for the day. This is done at the same time she prepares her spells. When the paladin first selects a creature for *divine troth,* that creature must be present and agree to receive the paladin's protection, but if the paladin already has a creature selected for *divine troth,* she can maintain that selection each day without her charge being present." ability2="Once per day, the divine guardian can cast ‹spell/locate creature› to find the current target of her *divine troth.*" order=a2 icon=protect-magic replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Guarding Hands (Su)]{icon=info-down info="The paladin can use *lay on hands* to heal herself or her *divine troth* as a @HLmove_action, but cannot use *lay on hands* to heal herself as a swift action. The divine guardian cannot use *lay on hands* more than once per round." alter="Lay on hands"}

::ab[Courageous Defense (Ex)]{l=3 order=an icon=protect-down ability="A divine guardian can intercept attacks directed at the target of her *divine troth* as if she had the ‹feat/Bodyguard› and ‹feat/In Harm's Way› feats." info="The divine guardian's *aura of courage* does not grant a bonus to allies within 10 feet, but still grants her immunity to fear effects." alter="Aura of courage"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l7="A divine guardian gains a bonus feat, chosen from the following list: ‹feat/Diehard›, ‹feat/Endurance›, ‹feat/Greater Shield Focus›, ‹feat/Greater Shield Specialization›, ‹feat/Heroic Defiance›, ‹feat/Heroic Recovery›, ‹feat/Missile Shield›, ‹feat/Ray Shield›, ‹feat/Saving Shield›, ‹feat/Shield Focus›, ‹feat/Shield Specialization›, or ‹feat/Tower Shield Proficiency›." l10="She gains a second bonus feat from the list above." l13="She gains a third feat from the list above." special="She must meet these feats' prerequisites, but for purposes of meeting the prerequisites of these bonus feats, she treats her paladin level as her fighter level, may use her Charisma score as her Dexterity score, and is considered to have the ‹feat/Spellbreaker› feat."}

divine_hunter.name: Divine Hunter
description: ## Divine Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/62›
Most paladins rush into battle, meeting evil toe-to-toe. The divine hunter prefers to engage evil from afar, striking down her foes before they can threaten her allies.
::ab[Precise Shot]{icon=power l1="A divine hunter gains ‹feat/Precise Shot› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." replace="Heavy Armor Proficiency"}

::ab[Shared Precision (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="When a divine hunter hits a creature with a ranged attack, she grants her allies within 10 feet of her the benefit of the Precise Shot feat against that target until the start of her next turn. Her allies must remain within 10 feet of her, and must be able both to see and hear the divine hunter to gain this benefit." replace="Aura of courage"}

::ab[Divine Bond (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A divine hunter forms a bond with her deity. This functions as the paladin's *divine bond* ability, except the bond must always take the form of a ranged or throwing weapon (excluding ammunition). In addition to the listed abilities, a divine hunter can add the ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/returning›, or ‹magic-enh/seeking› special abilities to her weapon, but she cannot add the *defending* or *disruption* special abilities." special="Special abilities added to throwing weapons function normally when the weapon is used in melee." replace="Divine bond"}

::ab[Distant Mercy (Sp)]{l=6 icon=aid ability="A divine hunter can expend two uses of her *lay on hands* abilities to use her *lay on hands* ability on a target within 5 feet per paladin level. *Distant mercy* has no effect on creatures harmed by positive energy, such as undead." replace="6th-level mercy"}

::ab[Aura of Care (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="A divine hunter and her allies gain a preternatural awareness of each other's position in battle. She and any allies within 10 feet of her no longer provide ‹rule/cover› against each other's ranged attacks unless they wish to. Allies who cannot move or otherwise take actions still provide cover, as do ‹rule/flat-footed› allies. The divine hunter and her allies must be able to see or hear each other to use this ability." replace="Aura of resolve"}

::ab[Hunter's Blessing (Su)]{l=11 icon=power swift="A divine hunter can expend a use of her *smite evil* ability to grant herself and all allies within 10 feet the ‹feat/Deadly Aim›, Precise Shot, and ‹feat/Improved Precise Shot› feats, even if they lack the prerequisites. The effects last for 1 minute." special="Evil creatures gain no benefit from this ability." replace="Aura of justice"}

::ab[Righteous Hunter (Su)]{l=14 icon=lower passive="A divine hunter's ranged weapons are treated as good-aligned for the purposes of overcoming damage reduction. Any ranged attacks made by an ally within 10 feet of her are likewise treated as good-aligned for the purposes of overcoming damage reduction." special="This ability functions only while the divine hunter is conscious." replace="Aura of righteousness"}

dusk_knight.name: Dusk Knight
description: ## Dusk Knight
‹SOURCE Blood of Shadows/18›

Some paladins focus their training to combat the shadows and use darkness to their advantage. Known as dusk knights, these paladins are primarily worshipers of ‹faith/Abadar›, but dusk knights who serve ‹faith/Iomedae› and ‹faith/Irori›, though rare, do exist.
::cskill{gain=stl lose=sm}

::ab[Shadow Smite (Su)]{icon=def-down ability="A dusk knight gains ‹rule/concealment› for 1 round per paladin level against the target of her *smite evil* ability in addition to its other effects. The dusk knight doesn't deal additional damage with her *smite evil* ability." alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Illuminating Zeal (Su)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="A dusk knight gains the ability to grant darkvision to her allies." standard="The dusk knight grants one target touched darkvision with a range of 60 feet for 1 hour. If the target already has darkvision, the range increases by 60 feet instead." special="If the dusk knight targets herself, using this ability is a @HLswift_action. Using this ability consumes two uses of her *lay on hands* ability." replace="Channel positive energy"}

::ab[Shadow's Embrace (Su)]{l=5 icon=power flavor="A dusk knight learns to fight effectively in areas of darkness." ability="She gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat and darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If she already has darkvision, the range increases by 30 feet instead." imp10="A dusk knight gains ‹feat/Improved Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." imp15="A dusk knight gains the ‹umr/see in darkness› ability." replace="Divine bond"}

::ab[Cloak of Shadow (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost flavor="A dusk knight becomes adept at moving through darkness." passive="She ignores her armor penalty when attempting Stealth checks and gains a bonus on Stealth checks equal to half her paladin level while in areas of ‹rule/dim light› or darkness." replace="Aura of resolve"}

::ab[Clouding Smite (Su)]{l=11 icon=lower flavor="A dusk knight's smite impairs her foe's abilities in the dark." ability="When a dusk knight successfully smites a foe, she reduces the target's darkvision by 60 feet for the duration of the smite. If the target has the *see in darkness* ability, it must succeed at a Fortitude save or lose this ability for the duration of the smite. The save DC is equal to 10 + 1/2 the dusk knight's paladin level + her Charisma modifier." replace="Aura of justice"}

empyreal_knight.name: Empyreal Knight
description: ## Empyreal Knight
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/62›
The empyreal knight dedicates her life to serving the celestial beings that guide mortals in their struggle toward the light.
::ab[Voices of the Spheres]{l=2 icon=power ability="An empyreal knight learns to speak and read Celestial, if she could not already." replace="Divine grace"}

::ab[Celestial Heart (Su)]{icon=def-power-protect flavor="As an empyreal knight advances, her ties with the celestial realms grant her a portion of the abilities and defenses of true ‹family/angel«s›." l3="She gains resistance 5 against acid, cold, and electricity." l6="She gains a +4 racial bonus on saves against poison." l9="Her defenses improve to resistance 10 against acid, cold, and electricity." l12="She gains immunity to petrification." l15="She gains *truespeech,* the ability to speak with any creature that has a language as though using a ‹spell/tongues› spell. This ability functions with a caster level equal to her character level, and is always active." l18="As a @HLswift_action, the empyreal knight can manifest a protective aura against evil. Against abilities or effects created by evil creatures, this ability provides a +4 deflection bonus to AC and a +4 resistance bonus on saving throws to anyone within 20 feet of her. It otherwise functions as a ‹spell/magic circle against evil›. She can use this ability for a maximum of 1 round per day per paladin level. These rounds do not need to be consecutive." replace=Mercy}

::ab[Celestial Ally (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic fullround="An empyreal knight can summon a celestial ally. This functions as ‹spell/summon monster I›, except it can only be used to summon ‹template/celestial› creatures, ‹family/archon«s›, and angels." incrementRoman="This improves to ‹spell/summon~4~2" incrementDesc=monster incrementEnd="›." usage="A number of times per day equal to the empyreal knight's Charisma modifier (minimum 1), but only one ally can be summoned at a time." replace="Lay on hands~channel positive energy"}

::ab[Divine Bond (Su)]{icon=power l5="An empyreal knight forms a bond with a mount, as the standard paladin ability." l8="Her mount gains the *celestial* template." l12="Her mount sprouts wings if it cannot already fly, and gains a fly speed of twice its land speed and good maneuverability. If the mount could already fly, its fly speed and maneuverability improve to at least this level." special="This ability otherwise functions as the paladin ability of the same name." alter="Divine bond"}

::ab[Empyreal Champion]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="An empyreal knight transcends her mortal self. Her DR increases to 10/evil. Her type is treated as ‹type/outsider› for the purposes of spells and magical effects. She gains darkvision 60 feet and low-light vision." standard="As a supernatural ability, she can sprout wings that allow her to fly at twice her land speed with average maneuverability. Any armor or clothing reshapes to allow her to fly when she uses this ability. She can retract the wings as a @HLfree_action." special="Unlike other outsiders, an empyreal knight can still be brought back from the dead as if she were a member of her previous creature type." order=ps replace="Holy champion"}

faithful_wanderer.name: Faithful Wanderer
description: ## Faithful Wanderer
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/18›

While most paladins stride brazenly forth to battle evil in gleaming armor, faithful wanderers understand that to accomplish real change in Abyssal environments, a paladin must learn to be self-sufficient and unobtrusive and that she must pick her battles carefully, lest her mission end abruptly in a blaze of glory.
::cskill{gain=kna~kp~per~stl~sur lose=diplo~ha~kno~ride}

::ab[Wanderer's Lore]{icon=skill-boost flavor="A faithful wanderer quickly learns that everything in the wastes is evil, and she develops the skills she needs to survive." info="A faithful wanderer gains a number of skill ranks equal to 4 + her Intelligence modifier at each level (instead of gaining a number of skill ranks equal to 2 + her Intelligence modifier)." alter="Skills per level" replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Hidden Aura (Su)]{icon=info-power-def info="A faithful wanderer doesn't have an *aura of good* like other paladins do, as this would give her presence away to hostile denizens of the Abyssal wastelands. Her other auras likewise don't reveal her presence. The benefits of her *aura of courage, aura of resolve, aura of faith,* and *aura of righteousness* affect only her." imp3="Her alignment aura emulates the ‹rule/alignment« planar» traits› of her current location (e.g. chaotic evil in the Worldwound or the Abyss) for the purposes of alignment-detection effects, though this doesn't change her actual alignment." imp8="She takes no penalties from the mildly chaos-aligned and mildly evil-aligned planar traits and treats all opposed strongly aligned planar traits as if they were only mildly aligned." imp11="She takes no penalties even in areas with strongly chaos- and evil-aligned planar traits." imp14="She leaves no trail in areas with chaos- and evil-aligned planar traits, can't be tracked in such terrain, and can use Stealth to hide in such terrain, even if the terrain doesn't grant cover or concealment." imp17="She can use Stealth to hide in areas with these planar traits even while being observed." replace="Aura of good~aura of justice" alter="Aura of courage~aura of resolve~aura of faith~aura of righteousness"}

::ab[Stalk Evil (Su)]{icon=boost ability="A faithful wanderer isn't interested in going out in a blaze of glory after exhausting a limited ability to *smite evil.* Instead, she has developed techniques to evade, stalk, and punish evil whenever necessary. She gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, Stealth, and Survival checks against evil outsiders, as well as a +2 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against them. These bonuses don't stack with ‹ability/favored enemy› bonuses." increment="p!These bonuses~0~5~4~2" imp7="The faithful wanderer also gains these bonuses against undead." imp13="She also gains these bonuses against evil dragons." replace="Smite evil"}

::ab[Champion's Bond (Su)]{icon=info-def-magic l5="A faithful wanderer must choose the weapon option of her *divine bond* class feature." l20="Whenever her *divine bond* is active, she gains several additional benefits, as follows: Her DR improves to 5/-. Additionally, if she rolls a natural 20 on an attack roll against an evil outsider and confirms the critical hit, the outsider is also subject to ‹spell/banishment›, using the faithful wanderer's paladin level as the caster level (her weapon and holy symbol automatically count as objects that the subject hates). Finally, whenever she uses *lay on hands* to heal herself, she heals the maximum possible amount of hit points." alter="Divine bond" replace="Holy champion"}

forest_preserver.name: Forest Preserver
description: ## Forest Preserver
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/70›
Forest preservers are sacred defenders of the woodlands and of the wild creatures and natural beauty within them.
::cskill{gain=climb~kna~sur lose=diplo~kno~sm}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A forest preserver gains the ranger's ‹ability/favored terrain› ability but must select either forest or jungle as her favored terrain." info="Text from the ability: *The ranger gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks when he is in this terrain. A ranger traveling through his favored terrain normally leaves no trail and cannot be tracked (though he may leave a trail if he so chooses).*" order=an increment="The bonuses granted by this ability~3~5~4~2" special="She does not gain additional favored terrains." replace="Aura of courage"}

::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A forest preserver (as well as her *divine bond* creature, if she has one) gains ‹ability/woodland stride›, as per the druid class feature." replace="Divine health"}

::ab[Sacred Botany (Ex)]{l=4 icon=learn info="A forest preserver adds the following spells to her spell list at the listed spell level." s1="Detect animals or plants~~entangle~~goodberry~~shillelagh" s2="Barkskin~~eagle eye~~tree shape~~warp wood~~wood shape" s3="Diminish plants~~plant growth~~snare~~speak with plants~~spike growth" s4="Antiplant shell~~awaken~ (trees only)~~blight~~command plants~~commune with nature~~thorn body~~tree stride~~wall of thorns" special="In addition, she treats her caster level as 1 higher when casting these spells in her favored terrain." replace="Channel positive energy"}

::ab[Fireproof Aura (Su)]{l=8 icon=def-protect order=pi passive="A forest preserver and creatures within 10 feet of her gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against fire effects and gain fire resistance 5; these are doubled for plant allies, which also gain ‹ability/evasion› against fire effects only." immediate="Whenever a fire spell would affect a creature within the *fireproof aura,* the forest preserver can expend three uses of her *lay on hands* ability to attempt to counter the effect, as if she had readied a ‹spell/dispel magic› spell (caster level = her paladin level) to counterspell." special="This ability functions only if the forest preserver is conscious." replace="Aura of resolve"}

::ab[Sacred Grove (Su)]{l=11 icon=magic ability="A forest preserver can expend two uses of her *smite evil* ability to consecrate a 20-foot-radius area that must contain a living tree. This fills the area with dense undergrowth, though it does not impede her allies' movement. In addition, animals, fey, and plants allied with the forest preserver gain a +1 sacred bonus on attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws, and animals, fey, and plants called or summoned within this area gain 1 hit point per Hit Die as long as the calling or summoning effect lasts or until the *sacred grove* effect ends (whichever comes first). If the area contains a shrine or hallowed area dedicated to a good-aligned deity (or at the GM's discretion, a place of natural magical power, such as a natural portal to the First World), these bonuses are doubled." ability2="When she uses this ability in her favored terrain, she can also imbue plants in the area with limited animation, allowing them to obscure vision and impede movement and attacks as if the area were affected by ‹spell/solid fog›." order=a2 special="A *sacred grove* remains enhanced in this way for a number of minutes equal to the paladin's level." replace="Aura of justice"}

::ab[Aura of Preservation (Su)]{l=14 icon=protect passive="A forest preserver grants animals and plants within 10 feet spell resistance equal to 11 + her paladin level. This spell resistance doesn't apply against harmless effects created by the forest preserver or allies within 10 feet of her. This ability functions only if the forest preserver is conscious." replace="Aura of faith"}

forgefathers_seeker.name: Forgefather's Seeker
description: ## Forgefather's Seeker
‹SOURCE Construct Handbook/13›

Torag constantly seeks those who would use the power of creation to threaten or harm others. To this end, he sends his seekers to locate constructs that are used for evil or that become uncontrollable. These paladins are adept at battling constructed foes and guarding those threatened by such creations.
::prereq{other="Must select ‹faith/Torag› as her patron deity."}

::ab[Detect Construct (Sp)]{icon=power ability="A Forgefather's seeker can detect the presence of constructs at will. This functions as the spell ‹spell/aura sight›, except the duration is 1 round and a Forgefather's seeker learns the locations and power of all auras of all constructs, similar to ‹spell/detect undead›." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Smite Construct (Su)]{icon=down-boost ability="This ability functions as the paladin's *smite evil* ability, but it functions only against constructs. Smiting a construct automatically bypasses any DR or hardness a construct might have." order=a2 ability2="A Forgefather's seeker can still *smite evil,* but she adds only half her Charisma bonus on attack rolls, adds only half of her level on damage rolls, and doesn't increase her bonus damage against outsiders with the evil subtype, evil-aligned dragons, or undead." special="This otherwise functions as *smite evil* for the purposes of feats, prerequisites, and other effects." alter="Smite evil"}
::ab[Aura of Destruction (Su)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A Forgefather seeker grants her allies *smite construct* with her *aura of justice* ability instead of *smite evil.*" alter="Aura of justice"}

::ab[Aura of Unmaking (Su)]{l=14 icon=lower passive="A Forgefather's seeker's weapons are treated as ‹eq-material/adamantine› for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. Any attack made against an enemy within 10 feet of her is treated as adamantine for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. This ability functions only while a Forgefather's seeker is conscious, not if she is unconscious or dead." replace="Aura of faith"}

::ab[Forgefather's Champion (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-aid-power flavor="A Forgefather's seeker becomes capable of completely dismantling constructs." passive="Her DR increases to 10/evil. In addition, whenever a Forgefather's seeker channels positive energy or uses *lay on hands* to heal a creature, she heals the maximum possible amount." ability="Whenever she uses *smite construct* and hits a construct, she can immediately attempt to destroy the construct. The construct is reduced to 0 hit points and destroyed unless it succeeds at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the Forgefather's seeker's level + the Forgefather's seeker's Charisma modifier). After this destruction effect and the damage from the attack is resolved, the *smite* immediately ends." order=pa replace="Holy champion"}

ghost_hunter.name: Ghost Hunter
description: ## Ghost Hunter
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/123›
Ghost hunters devote their lives to eliminating malevolent spirits and putting them to rest.

::ab[Ghostly Smite (Su)]{icon=down-boost ability="A ghost hunter's *smite evil* ability does not automatically bypass DR (other than DR/good). However, she treats her weapon as a ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapon against the target of her *smite.* She does not deal extra damage on the first attack against evil outsiders and evil dragons, but she deals 3 points of damage per paladin level she possesses to undead creatures on the first attack (instead of 2 points)." alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Exorcise Possession (Su)]{l=6 icon=magic-aid ability="A ghost hunter can try to end any possession spells or effects, or effects that directly exercise mental control over a creature. She must touch the affected creature (this requires a melee touch attack if the target is neither willing nor helpless) and expend a use of her *lay on hands* ability. The target can immediately attempt a Will save against the original save DC of the controlling effect. If the save is successful, the effect immediately ends, as if the target had succeeded at its original saving throw." replace="6th-level mercy"}

::ab[Speak to the Restless (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="A ghost hunter can commune with the spirits of the restless dead once per day. By holding a seance for 10 minutes, she can ask questions of a destroyed undead or a neutralized or destroyed ‹rule/haunt<s›. To use this ability on a corporeal undead, the ghost hunter must have the creature's head in her possession. For a haunt or incorporeal undead, this seance must be held in the haunt's location or where the incorporeal undead was destroyed. Treat this ability as ‹spell/speak with haunt› or as ‹spell/speak with dead› (but affecting only undead rather than only corpses), using the ghost hunter's class level as the caster level." replace="9th-level mercy"}

gray_paladin.name: Gray Paladin
description: ## Gray Paladin
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/69›

Gray paladins have discovered that in a world of intrigue, a strict code of honor hinders their options. Their divergence from the paladin's normal code still drains them, but somehow, they have found a way to avoid losing all of their powers.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=info info="A gray paladin can be lawful good, lawful neutral, or neutral good, though she must still follow a lawful good, neutral good, or lawful neutral deity." alter="Alignment restrictions"}
::cskill{gain=bluff~dis~intm}

::ab[Weakened Grace]{icon=down-info flavor="A gray paladin's loosened code weakens her connection to the power that grants her paladin abilities." imp2="She gains her first use of *smite evil* at 2nd level, instead of 1st." info="She never gains the *aura of good* or *divine grace* class features. Her *aura of courage* does not make her immune to fear, her *aura of resolve* does not make her immune to charms, and her *aura of righteousness* does not make her immune to compulsions." incrementOrd="She gains~1~3" incrementDesc=her incrementEnd=" use of *smite evil* per day." alter="Smite evil~aura of courage~aura of resolve~aura of righteousness" replace="Aura of good~divine grace"}

::ab[Code of Conduct]{icon=warning info="A gray paladin must be of lawful good, lawful neutral, or neutral good alignment and loses all class features except proficiencies if she ever willingly commits an evil act (for example, casting a spell with the evil descriptor). She should strive to act with honor and uphold the tenets of her faith, but failing to do so is not a violation of her code, and other than evil actions, she can do whatever else she feels is necessary to uphold the causes of law and good." replace="Code of conduct and associated abilities" alter=Ex-paladin}

::ab[Enhanced Health (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A gray paladin is far less a stranger to poisons than a normal paladin. She gains a +4 sacred bonus on all saving throws against poisons and diseases." replace="Divine health"}

::ab[Smite Foe (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A gray paladin can spend two uses of *smite evil* in order to smite a nongood creature that is not evil and gain her full benefits. The gray paladin must truly believe that the creature she smites is an opponent to the cause of good. Even if this nonevil foe is an outsider, undead, or dragon, she doesn't add increased damage to her first attack." replace="Channel positive energy" alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Aura of Subtlety (Sp, Su)]{l=11 icon=def-protect passive="A gray paladin learns how to protect herself from detection. She and her allies within 10 feet gain a +4 morale bonus on saving throws against divination effects. Furthermore, casters of divinations that target the gray paladin or her nearby allies must succeed at a caster level check as though the gray paladin and her nearby allies were under the effect of a ‹spell/nondetection› spell cast by the gray paladin, with a caster level equal to the gray paladin's paladin level." special="This ability functions while the gray paladin is conscious and while she is sleeping, but not if she is unconscious for any other reason, or dead." replace="Aura of justice"}

holy_guide.name: Holy Guide
description: ## Holy Guide
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/107›

A holy guide believes that it's his sacred calling to clear the roads of bandits between towns as well as to escort travelers to safety. He must enforce the rule of law in the wilderness and help those that cannot defend themselves against the many dangers of the area.
::cskill{gain=kg~sur}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost choice="A holy guide chooses a ‹ability/favored terrain› from the ranger favored terrains table. This otherwise functions like the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name." order=ca ability="Every time a holy guide would be able to select another *mercy,* he can instead select another favored terrain and increase his bonuses for one existing favored terrain, just like a ranger." replace="3rd-level mercy"}

::ab[Teamwork Feat (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A holy guide gains a ‹main/teamwork feat› as a bonus feat. He must meet the prerequisites for this feat." standard="He can expend one use of *smite evil* to grant this feat to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear him. Allies retain the use of this bonus feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every 2 levels the holy guide possesses. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of this bonus feat. Evil creatures do not gain the benefit of this teamwork feat, even if the paladin considers them allies." order=as replace="6th-level mercy"}

holy_gun.name: Holy Gun
description: ## Holy Gun
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/63›
Not all paladins are knights in shining armor. Holy guns roam the world searching for evil. And where they find it, they put it down.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power info="Holy guns are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, ‹main/equipment_>firearms›, and light armor."}

::ab[Have Gun]{l=1 icon=power-ranged ability="The holy gun gains the ‹feat/Amateur Gunslinger› feat and ‹feat/Gunsmithing› as a bonus feat. She also gains a battered gun identical to the one gained by the ‹class/gunslinger›." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Divine Deed]{head l=2 icon=power ability="The holy gun gains the following deed. This deed works and interacts with grit the same way as ‹ability/gunslinger deeds›, but only the holy gun can use it. If the holy gun also has levels in gunslinger, she can spend grit points from that class to use this deed." replace="Smite evil"}

::ab[Smiting Shot (Su)]{next icon=ranged standard="A holy gun can spend 1 grit point to make a *smiting shot* with a firearm attack. If the target is evil, the holy gun adds her Charisma bonus and her paladin level to the damage of the firearm attack. If the target of the *smiting shot* is an outsider with the evil subtype, an evil-aligned dragon, or an undead creature, the bonus to damage increases to the Charisma modifier plus 2 points of damage per level the paladin possess. Regardless of the target, *smiting shot* automatically bypasses any DR the creature might have."}

::ab[Divine Bond]{l=5 icon=power ability="A holy gun forms a bond with her deity. This functions as the paladin's *divine bond* ability, except the bond must always take the form of a firearm. In addition to the listed abilities, a holy gun can add the ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/reliable›, or ‹magic-enh/seeking› special abilities to her weapon, but she cannot add the *defending* or *disruption* special abilities." replace="Divine bond"}

::ab[Holy Grit (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A holy gun gains a number of grit points equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 1) and gains the use of a single gunslinger deed. She can select any deed that a 7th-level gunslinger could use." incrementOrd="The holy gun gains another point of grit and another gunslinger deed that~11~3~10~3" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd="-level gunslinger could use." special="If she already has levels in gunslinger, she gains a bonus to the maximum amount of grit she can have each day, equal to her Charisma bonus (if any) but gains no extra grit as the start of each day."}

::ab[Holy Slinger (Ex)]{icon=info info="This ability function like the *holy champion* paladin class feature, but the banishment occurs when she hits an evil outsider with the *smiting shot* deed." alter="Holy champion"}

holy_tactician.name: Holy Tactician
description: ## Holy Tactician
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/64›
The holy tactician inspires her allies on the field of battle. Her place is at their side against overwhelming odds, and her guidance brings out their true potential.

:::ab{icon=boost l=1 title="Weal's Champion (Su)" action="Swift Action" replace="Smite evil" special="She can grant this bonus against more than one creature at a time. To gain this benefit, the holy tactician's allies must be able to see or hear her, and she must be conscious." useInc=paladin~3~1 useF="1~1~3"}

A holy tactician can call on the powers of good to aid her against evil. Against evil targets, the holy tactician gains her Charisma bonus (if any) on her attack rolls against evil creatures, and if she hits, she adds 1/2 her paladin level on her weapon damage rolls as well. These bonuses last for 1 round for every two paladin levels the holy tactician has attained (minimum 1 round).

In addition, for 1 round after the holy tactician successfully strikes an evil creature, all non-evil allies within 30 feet of her gain a competence bonus on attack rolls equal to 1/2 her Charisma bonus against that creature as well as a competence bonus on damage rolls. The bonus on damage rolls is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of her paladin level.

::ab[Tactical Acumen (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A holy tactician gains a ‹main/teamwork feat› as a bonus feat." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these feats." repeat="She gains an additional bonus teamwork feat.~7~4" replace="Divine health~divine bond"}

::ab[Battlefield Presence (Su)]{l=3 icon=power standard="A tactician can direct her allies in battle, granting each ally within 30 feet one teamwork feat she possesses as a bonus feat. All allies must receive the same feat, but do not need to meet the prerequisites of this bonus feat." special="This ability does not function if the paladin is ‹rule/flat-footed› or unconscious. Allies must be able to see and hear the holy tactician in order to gain this benefit." swift="She may change the bonus feat granted." order=sw replace="Aura of courage"}

::ab[Guide the Battle (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power move="The paladin can direct her allies into an advantageous position once per round. Each ally (if physically able to) can move 5 feet without expending an action. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. The allies must be able to see or hear the paladin in order to make this movement and cannot be flat-footed." imp15="Her allies can make this 5-foot move and ignore ‹rule/difficult terrain›." replace="Aura of resolve"}

::ab[Weal's Wrath (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="A tactician can expend two uses of her *weal's champion* ability at once to enhance its effects. This functions as *weal's champion,* except the bonus she provides to her allies lasts until each creature she strikes is slain, her *weal's champion* ability expires, or the paladin herself is slain or knocked unconscious, whichever happens first." replace="Aura of justice"}

::ab[Masterful Presence (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-boost ability="A tactician gains the ability to grant a different bonus feat to each ally affected by her *battlefield presence.* In addition, all critical threats made by her and her allies against creatures affected by her *weal's champion* ability are automatically confirmed (no confirmation roll is needed)." replace="Holy champion"}

hospitaler.name: Hospitaler
description: ## Hospitaler
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/116›

Paladins are known for their charity and for tending to the sick. The hospitaler takes to this calling above all others, spending much of her time healing the poor, and giving aid and succor to those in need. The hospitaler has the following class features.
::ab[Smite Evil (Su)]{icon=down info="This functions as the paladin ability, with the adjusted advancement stated below." order=nu useInc=paladin~6~1 useF="1~1~6" alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Channel Positive Energy (Su)]{icon=aura l4="A hospitaler gains the ability to channel positive energy as a ‹class/cleric› equal to her paladin level - 3." special="Using this ability does not expend uses of *lay on hands,* as it does with other paladins." useMod=Charisma3 replace="Channel positive energy"}

::ab[Aura of Healing (Su)]{l=11 icon=aura-aid ability="A hospitaler can expend one use of her *channel positive energy* ability to emit a 30-foot aura of healing for a number of rounds equal to her paladin level. Allies in this aura (including the hospitaler) automatically ‹misc/stabilize› if below 0 hit points and are immune to ‹umr/bleed› damage." order=ap passive="Allies (including the paladin) that spend at least 1 full round inside the aura are healed an amount of damage equal to their total number of Hit Dice and may make a saving throw against any afflictions they are suffering from, such as a curse, disease, or poison. This saving throw only counts toward curing the affliction and does not impose any penalty on a failed save." special="Allies can only be healed once by a use of this ability and they can only attempt additional saving throws once per day, even if they are exposed to this aura multiple times." replace="Aura of justice"}

hunting_paladin.name: Hunting Paladin
description: ## Hunting Paladin
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/70›
Hunting paladins are tenacious trackers and stealthy stalkers in pursuit of evildoers.
::cskill{gain=kna~per~stl~sur lose=diplo~heal~kno~spc}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A hunting paladin is not proficient with heavy armor." alter="Armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Hunt Evil (Su)]{icon=boost-power-roll-def-warning ability="Once per day, a hunting paladin can designate a single evil creature within her sight or one that she has determined to be evil by declaring it the target of her hunt and following its tracks; this allows her to add her Charisma modifier as a bonus on Survival checks to follow that creature's tracks and on Perception and Stealth checks opposed by the target of her hunt. She can sense her target's presence and location, which lets her pinpoint her target's location when within 10 feet; if the target has ‹rule/concealment›, she can roll twice and use the better result on rolls to determine whether she misses due to the concealment. She gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge› as per the ‹class/barbarian› class feature against her target." special="If the hunting paladin targets a nonevil creature with *hunt evil,* the ability fails and it is wasted. This ability ends after 24 hours (unless she spends the next day's use of this ability to continue hunting the same target), after the hunting paladin or the target is killed or when she designates a new target for her hunt." replace="1st-level use of smite evil"}

::ab[Detect Evil (Su)]{icon=down-magic ability="A hunting paladin cannot use this ability as a move action. However, when a hunting paladin has successfully followed a creature's tracks for at least 1 minute, she can discern whether the tracked creature's alignment was evil, as if she had cast ‹spell/detect evil› in its presence and concentrated for 3 rounds." alter="Detect evil"}

::ab[Smite Evil (Su)]{icon=down l4="A hunting paladin gains this ability at 4th level, and it functions at her paladin level - 3. She can expend a daily use of *smite evil* to gain an additional daily use of *hunt evil.* She can use *smite evil* and *hunt evil* against the same target." alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn ability="For each level of spell she can cast, a hunting paladin can prepare and cast one spell per day from the ‹main/ranger spell« list› rather than the paladin spell list." alter=Spells}

::ab[Tireless Aura (Su)]{l=8 icon=def-protect passive="A hunting paladin becomes immune to ‹misc/fatigue› and sleep effects, and allies within 10 feet gain a +4 bonus on saving throws to avoid becoming fatigued or falling asleep." replace="Aura of resolve"}

invigorator.name: Invigorator
description: ## Invigorator
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/24›

Invigorators devote themselves to keeping their allies bodily whole. These selfless paladins believe the best way to combat evil is by bolstering the health and spirits of those who fight alongside them. They play vital roles and often command troops among martial faiths, such as those of ‹faith/Iomedae› and ‹faith/Torag›.

:::ab{l=1 incrementPlain="The DR~1~3" incrementEnd="/-." icon=def-protect useMod=Charisma3 action=Ability title="Bestow Hope (Su)" replace="Smite evil" imp7="The invigorator may choose a number of nonevil allies equal to her Charisma modifier + 1." imp20="The invigorator may choose a number of nonevil allies equal to her Charisma modifier + 2." special="The DR this ability grants does not stack with the DR the invigorator receives from *aura of righteousness* or *holy champion.*"}

An invigorator is a champion of her faith who can call upon her deity's holy power to bathe battling allies in protective energy. As a @HL[swift action] during combat, the invigorator can choose a number of nonevil allies within sight equal to her Charisma modifier upon whom to *bestow hope.* Once activated, *bestow hope* grants the invigorator and these allies DR 1/- until the combat ends, at which point the damage reduction immediately ends.

During a battle, the invigorator can change the allies this ability affects as a @HL[swift action]. Ending it is a @HL[free action], but each time it is activated during a single combat, another use of the ability is expended. The invigorator must participate in the combat to use this ability. If she is frightened, panicked, paralyzed, stunned, unconscious, or otherwise prevented from participating in the combat, any DR this ability bestows is temporarily suspended until she can participate in the combat again; this doesn't count as ending the ability.

::ab[Holy Fount (Su)]{l=11 icon=aid-aura free="An invigorator can expend two uses of *bestow hope* to radiate healing energies to bolster her nearby allies. For a number of rounds equal to 1/2 the invigorator's paladin level, the invigorator and all nonevil allies within a 10-foot radius of her gain ‹umr/fast healing› 10." special="This ability is an aura centered on the invigorator; allies must be within the aura at the beginning of their turns to benefit from the fast healing." replace="Aura of justice"}

::ab[Champion of Life (Sp)]{l=20 flavor="An invigorator's holy resolve can sometimes overcome death - or smite the horror that is undeath." icon=magic-boost standard="Three per day, an invigorator can use ‹spell/breath of life› as a spell-like ability, using her paladin level as her caster level. Creatures brought back to life with this ability do not suffer the normal temporary negative level the spell imposes." replace="Holy champion"}

iomedaen_enforcer.name: Iomedaen Enforcer
description: ## Iomedaen Enforcer
‹SOURCE Divine Anthology/6›

Since the publication of The Majestic Book of the Prime Ascended, certain paladins have taken "Prime Commander" to heart and have developed their own methods of upholding the teachings in this book. Iomedaean enforcers have altered abilities that allow them to stand against the forces of chaos. They seek to maintain order and uphold the laws of just rulers and governments, pursuing any who break those laws or otherwise disrupt the peace. Still, they remain lawful good servants of ‹faith/Iomedae›, and as such always seek to temper the justice they deliver with mercy.
::cskill{gain=intm~kl lose=diplo~kno}

::ab[Detect Chaos (Sp)]{icon=magic move="At will, an Iomedaean enforcer can use ‹spell/detect chaos›, as per the spell. The Iomedaean enforcer can concentrate on a single item or individual within 60 feet and determine whether it is chaotic, learning the strength of its aura as if she had studied it for 3 rounds. While focusing on one individual or object, the Iomedaean enforcer can't use *detect chaos* on any other object or individual within range." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Smite Chaos (Su)]{icon=info-boost ability="This ability functions as the paladin's *smite evil* ability, but against chaotic-aligned creatures. Smite chaos is twice as effective against chaotic-aligned aberrations, outsiders with the chaotic subtype, and fey." replace="Smite evil"}

::ab[Aura of Order]{l=14 icon=lower-aura passive="The paladin's weapons are treated as lawful-aligned for the purposes of overcoming damage reduction. Any attack made against an enemy within 10 feet of the Iomedaean enforcer is treated as if it were lawful-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." alter="Aura of faith"}

::ab[Armor of Law (Su)]{icon=info info="When an enforcer gains damage reduction from *aura of righteousness,* it is DR 5/chaos instead of DR 5/evil. When that DR increases from the *holy champion* class feature, it increases to DR 10/chaos." alter="Aura of righteousness~holy champion"}

iroran_paladin.name: Iroran Paladin
description: ## Iroran Paladin
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/39›

Iroran paladins meditate on self-perfection and train relentlessly, knowing that their example can inspire others to excel. ‹faith/Irori› offers no universal paladin code; each paladin in his service creates his own code as part of his spiritual journey, seeing the adherence to such a self-formulated creed as one of the many tests one must face to reach perfection.
::cskill{gain=acro~climb~swim}

::ab[Confident Defense (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="When wearing light or no armor and not using a shield, an Iroran paladin adds 1 point of his Charisma bonus (if any) per class level to his Dexterity bonus to his Armor Class. If he is caught ‹rule/flat-footed› or otherwise denied his Dexterity bonus, he also loses this bonus." replace="Proficiency with medium armor, heavy armor, and shields"}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{l=1 icon=power-melee ability="An Iroran paladin gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. In addition, he gains the unarmed strike ‹class/monk› ability, treating his monk level as half his paladin level (minimum 1) for calculating his unarmed strike damage."}
::ab[Aura of Law (Su)]{icon=info info="The power of an Iroran paladin's *aura of law* is equal to his class level." replace="Aura of good"}

::ab[Sense Perfection (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic ability="At will, an Iroran paladin can detect life forces as if he were using a paladin's *detect evil* class ability; however, instead of sensing evil creatures, he senses creatures that have a ki pool. Each such creature has an aura like a cleric of an aligned deity, and treats its current number of ki points as its Hit Dice for the purposes of this ability." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Personal Trial (Su)]{icon=boost swift="An Iroran paladin can declare one target within line of sight as his *personal trial.* The Iroran paladin gains an insight bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against that creature, to his AC against attacks made by the target, and on saving throws against the target's spells and special abilities. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of his paladin level." special="The *personal trial* effect remains until the target of the trial is dead or the next time the paladin rests and regains daily uses of this ability." useInc=paladin~3~1 useF="1~1~3" replace="Smite evil"}

::ab[Aura of Excellence (Su)]{l=3 icon=def-protect-roll passive="An Iroran paladin is immune to any effect that would force him to reroll a die against his will or roll twice and take the lower result. Whenever an ally within 10 feet of him would reroll a die against her will, she can roll twice and take the higher result. Whenever an ally within 10 feet would be forced to roll twice and take the lower result, she can instead roll three times and take the second-lowest result. This ability functions only while the Iroran paladin is conscious." replace="Aura of courage"}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic-lower ability="An Iroran paladin gains a ki pool with an effective monk level equal to his class level. The number of points in an Iroran paladin's ki pool is equal to 1/2 his class level + his Charisma modifier." special="By spending 1 point from his ki pool, he can ignore any damage reduction possessed by the target of his *personal trial* ability for 1 round. This ability otherwise functions as the monk ability of the same name." replace="Channel positive energy"}

::ab[Divine Body (Su)]{l=5 icon=info info="An Iroran paladin must form a bond with a weapon, except he can only choose to enhance his unarmed strike. This ability otherwise functions as *divine bond.*" replace="Divine bond"}

::ab[Aura of Perfection (Su)]{l=11 icon=roll passive="Whenever an Iroran paladin or ally within 10 feet would reroll a die and take the second result even if it is lower (via the good fortune ability of the ‹domain/Luck› domain, for example), he can roll an additional die as part of the reroll and use the higher of the two as the result of the reroll." special="This ability functions only while the Iroran paladin is conscious." replace="Aura of justice"}

knight_of_coins.name: Knight of Coins
description: ## Knight of Coins
‹SOURCE Merchant's Manifest/28›

Servants of gods who revere wealth, knights of coins promote fair trade and stable commerce between cities and between nations. Many of these knights are Abadarans, and some have also taken the sacred shield archetype. Regardless, knights of coins know that a prosperous city can uplift and enrich more people than pious prayers alone.
::cskill{gain=app~kl~per~umd}

::ab[Skills]{icon=info info="She gains a number of skill ranks equal to 4 + her Intelligence modifier at each level, instead of a number of skill ranks equal to 2 + her Intelligence modifier." alter="Skill ranks per level"}

::ab[Eye for Forgeries (Sp)]{icon=magic move="At will, a knight of coins can concentrate on a single item within 30 feet. When she does, the knight of coins can detect if it is magical, as per the spell ‹spell/detect magic›. The knight of coins cannot concentrate on more than one item in this way at a time." replace="Detect evil"}

::ab[Blessing of Prosperity (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost head flavor="A knight of coins is a patron of wealth and seeks to even the playing field for those who have unfair economic disadvantages." choice="At 3rd level, the paladin can select a *blessing* (see below).@FN1" order=csn info="A knight of coins who takes the ‹feat/Extra Mercy› feat can gain an extra *blessing of prosperity* instead of an extra mercy. Once a *blessing of prosperity* is chosen, it can't be changed. A character who has benefited from a *blessing of prosperity* cannot benefit from that same *blessing* again (whether bestowed by the same knight of coins or another knight of coins) for 24 hours." standard="By expending a use of *lay on hands,* the knight of coins can instead grant a boon to herself or a creature touched. These boons do not stack with themselves or with blessings from another knight of coins. A *blessing of prosperity* lasts up to 1 hour, though the knight of coins who bestowed it can end a *blessing's* benefits early (whether it affects her or another creature) as a @HLfree_action. Using this ability requires only one free hand and is a standard action, unless the knight of coins targets herself, in which case it is a @HLswift_action." special="If she knows multiple *blessings of prosperity,* the knight of coins can bestow multiple *blessings* on a single target as part of the action and the expenditure of a use of *lay on hands* required to use this ability. If she bestows multiple different *blessings,* these *blessings* are cumulative. For example, a 9th-level knight of coins' *blessing of prosperity* ability might grant a +4 sacred bonus on Appraise and Sense Motive checks as well as a +4 sacred bonus on Perception checks and to CMD against ‹rule/disarm› and ‹rule/steal› combat maneuvers." incrementOrd="She can select~3~3" incrementDesc=her incrementEnd=" *blessing.*" replace="3rd-, 9th-, and 15th-level mercies"}

::ab[Wealth]{next icon=boost passive="The target adds an extra 10% to the gp value gained when selling off treasure (normally 50% of the item's original value)." special="This blessing cannot result in selling treasure for more than 100% of its original value."}
::ab[Insight]{next icon=boost passive="The target gains a +4 sacred bonus on Appraise and Sense Motive checks."}
::ab[Seeing]{next icon=boost-def passive="The target gains a +4 sacred bonus on Perception checks and to CMD against disarm and steal combat maneuvers."}
::ab[Work]{next icon=boost passive="The target gains a +4 sacred bonus on Craft, Perform, and Profession checks."}
::ab[Seeking]{next icon=magic ability="The target can use ‹spell/locate object› as a spell-like ability once, using the knight of coins' class level as the caster level."}

::ab[Quality]{next icon=boost passive="The target can treat one ‹rule/settlement<s_in_play› as having its base value and purchase limit increased by 30%." special="The target must choose the affected settlement when the knight of coins bestows this blessing."}

::ab[Variety]{next icon=boost passive="The target can treat one settlement as being one size category larger for the purposes of determining available magic items." special="The target must choose the affected settlement when the knight of coins bestows this blessing."}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[The blessings were unnamed in the original text. They have been given names here for clarity.]{from=1}

kraken_slayer.name: Kraken Slayer
description: ## Kraken Slayer
‹SOURCE Blood of the Sea/15›

Triton kraken slayers oppose all underwater threats, but they especially target the tentacled monstrosities called ‹monster/kraken«s›. Kraken slayers develop specialized tactics to defeat their hated enemies as well as learn to recognize the signs of kraken activity in an area. A kraken slayer's greatest responsibility is to destroy evil; all other commitments fall by the wayside. Though kraken slayers uphold all promises they make, they must balance their obligations as kraken slayers with their role as knights and defenders of the ocean.

::ab[Smite Deepest Evil (Su)]{icon=down-power ability="This functions as the *smite evil* ability, but the kraken slayer does not get a bonus of 2 points of damage per level on the first successful attack against any creatures other than evil creatures with the aquatic or water subtype. She gains a bonus of 2 points of damage per level on all smite attacks made against evil creatures with the aquatic or water subtype." alter="Smite evil"}

::ab[Divine Immunity (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A kraken slayer is immune to poisons and diseases, including supernatural and magical diseases, delivered by the natural attacks of creatures with the aquatic or water subtype." replace="Divine health"}

::ab[Divine Bond (Sp)]{icon=info ability="A kraken slayer who chooses a weapon as her *divine bond* may either increase her weapon's enhancement bonus or add any of the following properties to her weapon: ‹magic-enh/axiomatic›, ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/grayflame›, ‹magic-enh/holy›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/merciful›, ‹magic-enh/seaborne›, and ‹magic-enh/speed›." order=a2 ability2="A kraken slayer who chooses a mount must select one suitable for an aquatic environment, such as an ‹companion/elasmosaurus›, ‹companion/giant seahorse›, or ‹companion/orca›." alter="Divine bond"}

::ab[Aura of Elusion (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost-def-protect passive="A kraken slayer gains a sacred bonus equal to her kraken slayer level on Escape Artist checks and combat maneuver checks to escape a grapple and to her CMD to avoid being grappled. Each ally within 10 feet of her gains a sacred bonus equal to half her kraken slayer level on Escape Artist checks and combat maneuver checks to escape a grapple and to CMD to avoid being grappled." special="This ability functions only while the paladin is conscious, not if she is unconscious or dead." replace="Aura of faith"}

legate.name: Legate
description: ## Legate
‹SOURCE Armor Master's Handbook/6›

The first legates in the Inner Sea region were ambassadors from Holomog, a celestial-worshiping confederation in southern Garund. Though they hold to the same strict paladin code, legates present themselves not as armed crusaders, but as peaceful envoys and advisors, bringing wisdom garnered from the long, stable history of their nation. They often serve as diplomats (and sometimes spies) for good organizations. Yet woe to those who believe them defenseless, for a legate can call upon her divine patron to provide her with armor and weapons.

::ab[Celestial Armor (Sp)]{icon=magic-def l=3 standard="A legate can conjure armor. This ability acts as the spell ‹spell/instant armor›, using her paladin level as her caster level, except as follows." repeat="The armor gains an additional +1 enhancement bonus.~6~3" special="These bonuses can be used to add any of the following armor special abilities: ‹magic-enh/champion›, ‹magic-enh/fortification› (heavy, light, or medium), ‹magic-enh/invulnerability›, or ‹magic-enh/spell resistance› (13, 15, 17, or 19).~~~In addition, the legate can grant her armor the ‹magic-enh/energy resistance› special ability at the cost of a +3 bonus, the ‹magic-enh/improved energy resistance› special ability at the cost of a +5 bonus, or the ‹magic-enh/righteous› special ability at a cost of a +4 bonus.~~~The armor must have least a +1 enhancement bonus before any special abilities can be added. The bonus and special abilities granted are determined when this ability is used and cannot be changed until this ability is used again." order=usn info="If the legate has a steed bond and is riding her steed, she can include her steed when summoning celestial armor, granting the steed barding with the same bonuses and special abilities (see below) as her own." usage="A number of times per day equal to her Wisdom bonus plus ‹misc/half› her paladin level." replace=Mercy}

martyr.name: Martyr
description: ## Martyr
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/64›

Every paladin's code instructs him to put the welfare of others before his own, but some paladins take this farther than most, shouldering the blackest suffering of the world onto themselves in order to help others and inspiring their allies to achieve heights of valor rather than seeking the glory for themselves.

::ab[Stigmata]{l=1 icon=down-boost useUnit=round useLMod=paladin~Charisma3 standard="The martyr can chant hymns of faith and cause bleeding stigmata to visibly appear on his body. While his *stigmata* are active, he takes 1 point of ‹umr/bleed› damage, which automatically ceases when he ends this ability but otherwise does not relent, even in the face of magical healing or Heal checks. His *stigmata* assist his allies, duplicating the effect of the ‹performance/countersong›, ‹performance/distraction›, or ‹performance/inspire courage› bardic performance of a bard of his paladin level, though both *countersong* and *distraction* use Heal skill checks instead of Perform skill checks." imp10="He can choose to duplicate the effects of ‹performance/inspire greatness›." imp16="He can choose to duplicate the effects of ‹performance/inspire heroics›." imp7="He can manifest *stigmata* as a @HLmove_action." imp13="He can manifest *stigmata* as a @HLswift_action." replace="Smite evil"}

::ab[See No Evil, Hear No Evil (Su)]{l=2 icon=def passive="The martyr and all allies within 20 feet of him gain a +4 morale bonus on saving throws against ‹ability/bardic performance›, sonic effects, language-dependent effects, and ‹umr/gaze› attacks." special="This ability functions only when the martyr is conscious." replace="Divine grace"}

::ab[Aura Mastery (Su)]{icon=boost-down ability="The martyr's *aura of courage, aura of resolve,* and *aura of righteousness* have a radius of 20 feet instead of 10 feet, but the martyr does not gain immunity to fear, charms, or compulsions from those abilities." alter="Aura of courage~aura of resolve~aura of righteousness"}

::ab[Aura of Health (Su)]{l=3 icon=def-protect passive="The martyr and all allies within 20 feet of him gain a +4 morale bonus on saving throws against diseases." special="This ability functions only when the martyr is conscious." replace="Divine health"}

::ab[Martyr's Mercy (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost-down ability="A martyr can apply any of the mercies for which he qualifies based on his paladin level to his *lay on hands,* even if he didn't select that mercy (or its prerequisites). However, when he uses a mercy he didn't select, he transfers the condition to himself for the remaining duration (ignoring any immunity he might have to the condition), and he can't use this ability if he already has that condition. If he transfers a condition to himself in this way, he can't remove it from himself with a personal-only ability." special="The martyr can use *lay on hands* on any ally within 30 feet, rather than needing to touch the ally, but using *lay on hands* on himself is a @HLstandard_action, not a swift action." alter="Lay on hands~mercy"}

mind_sword.name: Mind Sword
description: ## Mind Sword
‹SOURCE Occult Origins/20›
Veterans of mystical battles against demons of the Worldwound, mind swords merge psychic and divine power, probing and cleansing minds while their telekinetic blades cleave demonic flesh.

::ab[Mind Arsenal (Su)]{icon=ranged l2="A mind sword can make a telekinetic attack with a melee weapon. This functions as the *hand of the apprentice* ‹arcaneschool/universalist› wizard school ability, but any calculations of that ability based on Intelligence are instead based on Charisma." l6="A mind sword can expend two uses of this ability as a @HLfull-round_action to attack the same opponent multiple times, as if using the full-attack action." imp12="A mind sword can expend one use of this ability as a @HLfull-round_action to combine melee attacks and ranged attacks aimed at different targets within 60 feet as part of a single full-attack action." replace="Lay on hands"}

::ab[Touch Treatment (Su)]{icon=aid head l3="A mind sword can expend one use of her *mind arsenal* ability to remove minor harmful mental conditions, as per the ‹class/mesmerist› *touch treatment* class feature (minor ability)." l9="Her *treatment* expands to the &quot&moderate&quot& version of the ability." l15="She can remove conditions as per the &quot&greater&quot& ability of *touch treatment.*" replace=Mercy}

::ab[Touch Treatment (Su)]{flavor="Text from the mesmerist ability." next icon=aid l3="You can help allies shake off harmful conditions - especially those that affect their minds - by channeling psychic energy through your healing hands. You must be able to touch your target. You can remove the *fascinated* or *shaken* condition from your target each time you use this ability. This is the &quot&minor&quot& ability." imp6="You can now also remove the *confused, dazed, frightened,* and *sickened* conditions. This is the &quot&moderate&quot& ability." imp10="You can now also remove the *cowering, nauseated, panicked,* and *stunned* conditions. This is the &quot&greater&quot& ability." imp14="You can instead expend one use of touch treatment to replicate the effects of ‹spell/break enchantment›. This targets only one creature you are touching and follows all other restrictions of touch treatment."}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn info="A mind sword adds the following psychic spells to her paladin spell list at the listed spell levels." s1="Detect psychic significance~~mage hand~~telekinetic projectile~~thought shield I" s2="Apport object~~enshroud thoughts~~mental barrier I~~thought shield II" s3="Intellect fortress I~~mental barrier II~~telekinetic maneuver~~thought shield III" s4="Intellect fortress II~~telekinesis~~thought shield IV~~thoughtsense~~tower of iron will I" special="The mind sword can prepare and cast these psychic spells as divine paladin spells. However, the mind sword cannot use these spells for item creation, including making potions or scrolls of these spells." replace="Channel positive energy"}

pearl_seeker.name: Pearl Seeker
description: ## Pearl Seeker
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/54›

Some paladins are called to their faith by strange empathic visions and dreams that beckon them to the oceans and beyond, to right aquatic injustice and search for a glorious gasping and whispering pearl they don't understand. Their contact with the strange but benevolent entity sending the dreams grants them unusual powers compared to other paladins.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A pearl seeker is proficient with light and medium armor but not with shields." alter="Armor proficiency"}
::cskill{gain=swim}

::ab[Seek Impressions (Ex, Sp)]{flavor="Pearl seekers gain their power from a rare resonant sensitivity to the vision-granting entity." icon=power ability="A pearl seeker gains ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as a bonus feat and can use ‹spell/detect psychic significance› at will as a spell-like ability." replace="Detect evil"}
